skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill wh...
skill.author: AJV009

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found false positives caused by design-related wording in SKILL.md. No code execution, network access, filesystem access, environment access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm usage on design-focused text. The referenced lines contain skill metadata and frontend design guidance, not cryptographic APIs, hashes, encryption routines, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of SKILL.md confirms the flagged terms appear only in prose about frontend aesthetics. No cryptographic operation or implementation exists in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance patterns in frontend design prose. The referenced lines describe interface goals, typography, and visual details; they do not invoke host inspection commands or collect system data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is descriptive Markdown guidance. No commands, APIs, or instructions request operating system enumeration or data collection.

content.value_statement: Generic interface prompts often produce bland layouts and repeated visual patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished, context-specific frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of a clear aesthetic direction before implementation.
Encourages production-grade HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or Vue interfaces.
Provides concrete guidance for typography, color, motion, spacing, and backgrounds.
Pushes generated interfaces away from common AI design defaults.
Supports both maximalist and minimal interface directions.
limitations: Does not include reusable components, scripts, or design assets.
Does not provide accessibility testing or automated visual QA.
Relies on the host AI tool to implement and verify frontend code.
May need project-specific design system constraints from the user.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Generate a polished app screen with a strong visual direction and working frontend behavior.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a Prototype
use_cases.description: Turn a plain mockup into a more memorable interface while preserving functional requirements.

use_cases.title: Explore Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Compare distinct aesthetic treatments for the same page, component, or application concept.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Single Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused UI element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-design to create a distinctive pricing card component for a SaaS analytics product. Include responsive layout and polished interaction states.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Page Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a complete page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-design to build a landing page for a boutique fitness studio. Choose a bold aesthetic direction and implement the first screen.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with existing product constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-design to improve this dashboard without changing its data model. Make it clearer, more distinctive, and easier to scan.

prompt_templates.title: Create Design Variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced exploration across multiple visual approaches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use frontend-design to propose three different aesthetic directions for this editor interface, then implement the strongest option.

output_examples.input: Create a homepage for an independent architecture studio.
output_examples.output: A full-page concept with editorial typography, controlled asymmetry, textured backgrounds, and responsive sections for projects and studio details.
A refined dashboard direction with clearer hierarchy, stronger spacing, purposeful color accents, and interaction states for key controls.

output_examples.input: Design a mobile onboarding flow for a finance app.
output_examples.output: A concise onboarding sequence with distinctive typography, confident visual rhythm, accessible contrast, and polished transition guidance.

best_practices: Provide audience, brand tone, technical stack, and must-have states before implementation.
Ask for one clear aesthetic direction when consistency matters more than exploration.
Review generated output for accessibility, responsiveness, and project design system fit.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for user research or product strategy.
Do not request vague beauty improvements without context or constraints.
Do not skip verification of generated frontend code in the target application.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create distinctive frontend components, pages, and app interfaces with stronger visual direction.

faq.question: Does it generate working code?
faq.answer: It instructs the AI assistant to implement working frontend code when the host tool supports code editing.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this a design system?
faq.answer: No. It is guidance for creating tailored interfaces, not a reusable component library.

faq.question: Can it follow an existing brand?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide brand rules, colors, typography, examples, and constraints in the prompt.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not require network access.
